headline: Creativity is a habit of preparation
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp in The Creative Habit said it plain—
Creativity ain't lightning; it's preparation trained.
Habit over hype... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Creativity is a habit of preparation, not a strike of luck,
Show up before the spark, stack the clay, build the truck.
Talent without practice is a rumor in the dark,
Prep the room, set the tools, light the match, leave a mark.
No muse on payroll, no vibe-check gate—
You earn the flow by showing up early, never late.
Habit is the engine, inspiration rides shotgun,
Prepare like a pro till the work gets done.
[Verse 1]
Tharp's a choreographer—body memory, studio floor,
She built ballets the same way: drill, then explore.
Creativity as ritual, not a mood that appears,
The blank space fills when you train the gears.
She talks preparation like athletes talk the gym—
Warm-up, structure, constraints that slim the whim.
Not waiting for genius to kick the door in,
You arrange the conditions so the idea can pour in.
Lineage of dancers who rehearse till the bone,
Writers with morning pages, painters alone
Before the gallery lights, before the ovation—
The work is the prep, the prep is the vocation.
Lightning is rare; the habit is daily bread,
Stack small days till the big day is fed.
That's Tharp's thesis in a hard-earned frame:
Make creativity a practice with a name.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: phone face-down at 6 a.m.,
Open the draft before the Slack tsunami hits again.
AI can spit a sketch—cool, but you still design the brief,
Prompt is prep, edit is craft, habit is the chief.
Side project? Calendar block beats "someday" every time,
Kid's homework table becomes your sketch-pad crime.
Stand-ups and sprints at work? Same Tharp muscle flex—
Prep the agenda, clear the clutter, then flex the next.
Gym bag by the door, laptop charged, notes in one app,
You don't wait for motivation; you close the gap.
TikTok wants the climax; real makers want the reps,
Twenty minutes raw before the feed intercepts.
Parenting chaos? Micro-prep: one idea card tonight,
Tomorrow you expand it when the house is light.
Creativity is muscle memory, not a lucky scroll—
Prepare the runway, then let the takeoff roll.
[Bridge]
Mood is weather; habit is climate...
Show up dull days—that's how you prime it...
Prep is love in work clothes, quiet and true...
The spark finds people already mid-move.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So tomorrow: one ritual, under two minutes, no debate—
Open the file, stretch the body, clear one plate.
Name the habit after Tharp, own the prep like rent,
Inspiration can visit once the work is spent.
No more "I need to feel it"—feelings follow form,
Build the creative habit, weather every storm.
Preparation first, thunder later if it comes—
But you still ship the dance when the music drums.
Habit of prep—that's the real creative crown.
Show up tomorrow. Put the lightning down.

headline: Ritual is a leap into the creative zone
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp, The Creative Habit—listen close—
Ritual is a leap into the creative zone, not a pose.
Same door, same cue... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Ritual is a leap into the creative zone,
A trigger that tells your brain: you're not alone.
Same walk, same coffee, same open of the page—
The body leads, the mind quits the cage.
Don't wait for the zone; the ritual is the key,
One consistent cue, then the work runs free.
Leap in on purpose, leave the mood behind—
Ritual is the on-switch for a focused mind.
[Verse 1]
Tharp cabbed to the gym every day—same ride, same start,
That ritual was the doorway into art.
Not superstition—signal: now we create,
The body crosses first, the mind follows late.
Creative zone isn't magic; it's a trained switch,
Repetition builds the bridge from life to stitch.
Dancers bow in, writers light a lamp,
Scientists clear the desk—ritual is the stamp.
She teaches: invent a ceremony you can keep,
Small enough for bad days, strong enough for deep.
Once the leap is automatic, resistance thins,
You're already working before doubt begins.
History of makers: Picasso's hours, King's desk light,
Same threshold crossed from ordinary night.
Ritual says: this is sacred time, protect the frame—
Then the zone arrives without needing a name.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: AirPods in, playlist one track long,
Open Notion, close Discord—that's your song.
Laptop to the cafe corner seat you claim each week,
Phone in airplane, water filled—no doomscroll peek.
Remote job? Start stand: stretch, pour tea, timer set,
Zoom later; first twenty is for the real bet.
School drop-off done—backpack hits the floor, then desk,
That sequence is the leap; the rest is less stress.
AI session? Ritual: paste the constraints first, then generate,
Don't chat forever—structure the date.
Gym before code, walk around the block before the pitch,
Same shoes by the door—habit makes you rich
In attention currency, the only scarce stock,
Ritual is the vault door with a simple lock.
Kids nap? Ritual clock: open the canvas now,
Leap in dirty and tired—still keep the vow.
[Bridge]
The zone is not a place you hunt...
It's a door you build and front...
One cue, one breath, one start...
Ritual is how the work finds your heart.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So name your leap tonight—one trigger, no thrills,
Same seat, same app, same first three drills.
Twyla said the cab was enough to flip the switch—
Your version: a kettle, a stretch, a page, a niche.
Tomorrow when resistance talks, don't argue the case,
Do the ritual first; the zone takes the place.
Leap into the creative zone on purpose, not luck—
Ritual is the leap. Take it. Don't get stuck.

headline: Scratching gathers raw material before form
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp in The Creative Habit scratched first—
Gather raw material before form, feed the thirst.
Scratch, don't sculpt yet... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Scratching gathers raw material before form,
Messy notes, odd clips, fragments in a storm.
Don't demand the masterpiece on line one,
Fill the well, then shape when the gathering's done.
Scratch is research with your hands and eyes,
Collect before you polish, surprise before disguise.
Raw first, structure second—that's the craft creed,
Scratch wide, then choose what the piece will need.
[Verse 1]
Tharp's "scratching" is hunting without a final plan—
Photos, walks, overheard lines, a scrap of a man
Moving weird on the subway—file it, don't judge,
Creative work starts with a generous grudge
Against blank perfection. You stockpile stimuli,
Dance steps half-formed, newspaper, lullaby.
Form comes later when the pile has weight,
Then you sort, reject, and elevate.
It's not procrastination; it's intentional forage,
Like a chef before the menu, a scout before the storage.
Lineage: scrapbooks, mood boards before Pinterest names,
Composers humming nonsense into future frames.
Scratching says: quantity of input, then design,
Trust the pile; the pattern will align.
Skip scratching and you force a thin idea thin—
Start with abundance, then carve the win.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: voice-memo the shower thought,
Screenshot the ad that hit, bookmark the plot
In a random podcast—don't "use it" yet, just store,
One folder labeled SCRATCH, open it before
You open Figma or the pitch deck polish pass.
TikTok rabbit holes? Curate ten, don't mass
Copy the trend; steal textures, colors, cuts,
Then leave them raw till your own concept ruts.
AI dumps a hundred options—scratch, not finish,
Highlight three lines, trash the rest, diminish
The urge to ship the first pretty draft as gospel.
School project: sticky notes on the wall, then gospel.
Parenting scrap: kid's phrase on the fridge is research,
Side hustle: competitor screenshots in a church
Of ugly truth—before your brand voice locks.
Scratch this week; form next week. Unbox the blocks.
[Bridge]
Form without scratch is a house on sand...
Scratch without later form is a never-land...
Gather first—permission to be rough...
Then edit like a sculptor: enough is enough.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Cut the habit of "must look finished by noon,"
Spend thirty minutes scratching—ugly, loose, soon.
Twyla's pile becomes ballet; your pile becomes product,
Or poem, or lesson plan—trust the conduct.
Raw material first: photos, quotes, bad sketches, clips,
Tomorrow: open the scratch folder, make three trips
Into chaos, then pick one thread to form.
Scratch gathers. Then you transform.

headline: Box research physically per project
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp—The Creative Habit—boxes on the shelf—
One physical box per project: research that proves itself.
Contain the chaos... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Box research physically per project, claim the space,
One container, one mission, one working place.
Clippings, notes, objects, maps—throw the fuel in,
When the box is full of life, the piece can begin.
Don't scatter ideas across a dozen apps half-dead,
Give each project a home for the stuff in your head.
Physical or digital twin—same rule applies:
One box, one focus, watch the work rise.
[Verse 1]
Tharp kept a cardboard box for every dance she made,
Threw in tickets, fabric swatches, articles that stayed
Relevant to the theme—memory externalized,
The box was a second brain that never lied.
When stuck, she opened it; when done, she closed the lid,
Projects don't blur when their research is hid
In separate shells. Commitment made visible,
A pile that says: this is real, not divisible
From half-thoughts floating. Creators need edges,
Boundaries that hold the wild while the mind pledges
To one idea long enough to grow roots.
Lineage of ateliers: drawers, portfolios, boots
With mud from the research walk—proof you went out,
Not just sat and fretted in a spiral of doubt.
The box is a contract: I will fill this thing,
Then make from what's inside—that's the ring.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: one Drive folder per client name,
Or a real shoebox under the desk—same game.
Phone-free hour: title the calendar with the box title,
Show up, add one artifact—photo, quote, recital
Of a competitor's bad ad, a texture from a walk,
A PDF, a kid's drawing if the theme can talk.
Slack channels per project beat DMs that drown,
Notion page with a cover image as the crown
Of your digital box—still one home, not twelve tabs.
Side hustle: Ziploc of samples, stickers, scraps.
AI chats? Export the best threads into the box,
Don't leave genius buried in chat history locks.
School science fair: literal bin of prototypes,
Parent project: envelope of recipes and types.
Book the hour, fill the box, even if mood is gone—
Tharp's lesson: the container keeps you on.
[Bridge]
Scatter is the enemy of depth...
A box is a promise you kept...
Open it when you're lost...
Close it when you ship the cost.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: name one project, make one box—real or file—
Drop three things in before you chase the style.
Phone-free hour titled with that insight on the calendar wall,
Show up empty-handed? Still show—add nothing's not the call;
Add something small: a sentence, a leaf, a link, a bone.
Box research physically per project—claim your zone.
When the box feels alive, start building from the stash.
One project. One box. Make it cash—or make it art. Your stash.

headline: Skill is the backbone of improvisation
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp, The Creative Habit—hear the spine—
Skill is the backbone of improvisation, not divine.
Train so you can freestyle... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Skill is the backbone of improvisation,
You freestyle free because you drilled the foundation.
No craft, no wings—just chaos dressed as cool,
Build the muscle first, then break every rule.
Improv looks like magic to the untrained eye,
Underneath: repetitions that taught you how to fly.
Spine of skill, then bend—that's the real jazz,
Prepare the body so the moment has a path.
[Verse 1]
Tharp's dancers improvise on top of technique stacked,
Without turnout, without balance, the "free" falls flat.
Jazz greats knew scales before the solo soared,
Athletes run patterns till the game feels adored
By instinct. Creativity's same physics, same law:
Spontaneity is trained, not a flawless draw
From nowhere. She pushes practice as the license
To invent live—skill is the silent science.
History: ballet barre before the avant-garde leap,
Writers with grammar then shatter it deep.
Picasso could draw classical before cubism cracked—
Freedom is a graduate of structure, exact.
Don't romanticize "winging it" as purity,
Empty skill is noise; empty prep is insecurity.
Backbone first: scales, drills, forms, reps, code kata—
Then improvise like you mean it. That's the data.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: before the jam session stream,
Practice the transitions offline, build the team
Of muscle memories—Figma shortcuts, guitar chords,
Excel models till the what-if board affords
Real-time answers in the meeting without sweat.
AI improv? Prompt skill is still a skill set—
Learn constraints, evals, version control of thought,
Then riff with the model; don't pray it be caught
Holding your career. Kids' sports: fundamentals daily,
Then game-day creativity looks crazy-gaily.
Stand-up open mic: write first, then free-associate,
Kitchen riff on recipes after you can plate.
Cut the habit of "I'll just vibe through the demo,"
Half the time you're exposing the hollow memo.
This week: thirty focused skill minutes, then play ten—
Spine then bend. Improv will thank you then.
[Bridge]
Freedom without skill is a stumble...
Skill without freedom is a mumble...
Stack them—backbone, then the leap...
Improv is the harvest of what you keep.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Name one skill that props your freestyle claim,
Drill it half as much as you scroll the fame.
Tharp: improvisation stands on practiced feet—
Tomorrow: scales, drills, reps—then freestyle heat.
Skill is the backbone; bend it, don't pretend.
Train hard. Then invent. That's the end—and the begin.

headline: Every piece needs a spine—the governing idea
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp—The Creative Habit—hold the line—
Every piece needs a spine: the governing idea, defined.
One idea that holds... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Every piece needs a spine—the governing idea,
One strong bone the whole body can adhere.
Without it, pretty parts just wander and fray,
With it, every choice knows what to obey.
Spine first, ornaments second—don't reverse the stack,
Name the core, then build, never look back
Till the work can stand when you strip the flair—
If the idea still holds, the piece is there.
[Verse 1]
Tharp demands a spine for dances, stories, shows—
Not a mood board mush where anything goes.
The governing idea is the thesis in motion,
A sentence you can test against every notion:
Does this step serve the spine? Keep it. No? Cut.
Editors know this; architects know the strut.
A ballet about obsession needs choices that tighten,
Not random beauty that leaves the theme frightened.
Lineage: themes in novels, motifs in symphonies,
Brand platforms that say one thing in companies.
Without a spine you decorate a void,
Audience feels it—even if they're overjoyed
By moments, they leave with nothing to hold.
Tharp's craft: articulate the core in cold
Clear words before the spectacle expands—
Then let spectacle serve what the spine commands.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: pitch deck with one sentence north,
Every slide either proves it or gets thrown forth
Into the trash. App feature creep? Ask the spine:
Does dark mode serve the job-to-be-done design?
TikTok series: one promise per account, not twelve personas,
School essay: thesis line before the quotes and cornerstones.
AI can generate infinite pretty branches—
Your job: paste the governing idea in the stances
Of every prompt. Parenting project weekend: one goal,
Not five half-done crafts that scatter the soul.
Twenty-five-minute block: restate the spine in twelve words max,
Then only actions that obey those tracks.
No posting till the core can stand alone—
Likes don't replace a spine made of bone.
Name it on a sticky, tape it to the monitor edge,
When shiny objects call, show them the pledge.
[Bridge]
Ornament without spine is a costume on air...
Spine without craft is a sermon bare...
Hold both—core and skill in tandem...
Governing idea: the quiet anthem.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Restate your piece's spine before you touch the keys,
Twelve words max—then work twenty-five minutes that agrees.
Twyla: every piece needs that governing idea tight—
Strip the flair tonight; if it still stands, you're right.
Spine first tomorrow. Cut what doesn't serve.
Governing idea. Hold the nerve.

headline: Failure is data for the next attempt
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp, The Creative Habit—no shame loop—
Failure is data for the next attempt, not a soup
Of self-hate... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Failure is data for the next attempt,
Not a verdict on your soul—just a sample you exempt
From drama. Read the wreck, extract the clue,
Adjust the plan, run the trial new.
Fall, log, learn, retry—that's the lab mind,
Creative life is iterative by design.
Don't worship the flop; don't deny it either—
Harvest the failure, come back meaner, cleaner, wiser.
[Verse 1]
Tharp's studio is full of steps that didn't work,
She treats collapse as research, not a personal quirk
Of doom. Choreography fails in public light,
Then gets rebuilt with sharper insight.
Creators who hide failure freeze their growth chart,
Creators who study it restart with art.
Lineage: Edison bulbs, scientific method bare,
Athletes watching tape of the miss, the air
Where the pass should've been. Dance is experiment—
So is writing, product, painting, every attempt.
She won't romanticize pain; she'll instrumentalize it,
Turn the bruise into a better visit
To the problem. Ego wants a perfect streak;
Craft wants honest notes from the week.
Failure is tuition if you pay attention—
Ignore the invoice and you learn detention.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: ship the feature, read the metrics cold,
Churned users aren't villains—they're data gold.
AI output wrong? Save the bad run, label why,
Improve the prompt; don't just curse the sky.
Job interview flop: write three lines what you'd change,
Not a week of rumination in the shame range.
Kid fails a test—study the gaps, not the identity hit,
Side project launch with five sales? That's a kit
Of feedback: copy, price, channel, timing, trust.
Gym PR missed: sleep, form, load—adjust.
Post that flopped on LinkedIn? A/B the hook next dawn,
Don't delete your courage with the post that's gone.
Twenty-five minutes: restate "failure is data," then retry
One small version smarter—no public cry needed, just apply.
Cut the doomscroll after a miss; open the notes app instead,
Three bullets: what broke, what to test, what's dead.
[Bridge]
Shame is a closed loop...
Data is an open door...
Choose the lab over the courtroom floor...
Next attempt is what the last one was for.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: one attempt that might fail on purpose light—
Then write the data before you sleep that night.
Twyla's truth: the next try is the point of the fall—
Failure is data. Run the protocol.
No drama spiral. Log. Adjust. Attempt again.
That's how creative habits win.

headline: Collaboration needs clear roles
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp—The Creative Habit—crew on stage—
Collaboration needs clear roles, or you get cage
Match energy with chaos... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Collaboration needs clear roles—who decides, who does,
Who owns the finish line, who just buzzes.
Talent in a pile without a map is noise,
Name the jobs, share the spine, then deploy the poise.
Not less creative—more free inside the frame,
Clear roles let each artist play their game.
Credit, veto, timeline, lane—write it once,
Then make something together that could never come from one.
[Verse 1]
Tharp knows ensemble: dancers, designers, light, sound—
Without role clarity, rehearsals break down.
Collaboration isn't "everyone does everything cute,"
It's interlocking skills with a dispute
Path when visions clash. Who holds final say?
Who proposes, who refines, who walks away
From ego for the piece? She values the team
But refuses romantic fog as a scheme.
Theater history: stage manager, director, cast—
Blur those lines and the show won't last.
Jazz bands: solo space and rhythm section law,
Creative groups die from unspoken seesaw.
Clear roles are respect in structural form,
Not bureaucracy—oxygen in the storm.
Make the invisible contracts visible ink,
Then the art can breathe and the friendship won't sink.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: startup of three friends, no titles—
Six months in, resentment over "who writes the recitals"
Of the product story. Fix: RACI on one page,
Or simple: you ship backend, I own the stage
Of customer voice, she owns design system true.
Group project at school: assign before you do
The slides—researcher, editor, presenter, checker.
Slack: channel owners, not @channel forever wrecker.
Marriage creative project: who books, who builds, who posts—
Unsaid assumptions are how good love ghosts
The side hustle. AI collab with a human pair:
Human sets taste and ethics; model drafts the air—
Still need a human owner of the final call.
Remote team: decision log beats meeting sprawl.
This week: make one small finished thing that only lives
If roles were clear in how your team gives.
[Bridge]
Friendship isn't a role map...
But work needs one on the lap...
Love the people; define the lanes...
That's how collab remains.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: write three roles for one joint project bare—
Owner, maker, reviewer—who stands where.
Twyla: collaboration needs clear roles to thrive—
Ambiguity is where good teams dive
And drown. Surface the contracts. Finish something real
That only exists because the roles could seal.
Clear roles. Shared spine. Then make it sing.
Crew up right—or don't crew the thing.

headline: Scale practice when life interrupts—do not quit
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp, The Creative Habit—life will hit—
Scale practice when life interrupts—do not quit.
Shrink the session, keep the streak... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Scale practice when life interrupts—do not quit,
Five minutes still counts when the calendar splits.
Don't all-or-nothing your creative vow,
Reduce the load, keep the thread alive somehow.
Interrupted seasons are part of the craft path,
Quit is the only size that does the math
Of zero forever. Scale down, stay in—
Small practice beats a perfect plan you bin.
[Verse 1]
Tharp lived tours, injuries, age, and time's squeeze—
She teaches maintenance mode, not fantasies
Of endless perfect hours. When life invades,
You don't abandon the art; you change the grades
Of intensity. Ten pliés instead of two hours,
One page instead of a chapter's powers.
The habit's identity is continuity,
Not hero days that collapse in scrutiny
Of real life. Creative people fail by binary:
"Can't do full—so I do none." That's the adversary.
Lineage of makers with day jobs and kids,
Night notebooks, weekend scraps, humble bids
On stolen minutes. The spine of practice bends,
It doesn't snap unless you choose the end.
Scale is wisdom; quit is a story you sell
Yourself when tired. Tharp says: still show, still tell.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: newborn week—write three lines, not novels,
Deadline hell at work—stretch ten minutes, no full models.
Travel day: voice-memo ideas in the Uber queue,
Sick on the couch: read one craft page, still a move.
Phone-free hour booked—if life steals fifty minutes, keep ten,
Show up even if the mood is a ghost again.
AI can hold a stub for you: paste yesterday's note,
Add one sentence; that's scaled practice, not a quote
For Instagram. Parent-teacher night? Sketch on the program edge.
Layoff season? Small portfolio pieces, not a pledge
You can't keep of "rebuild everything by May."
Gym closed? Bodyweight. Studio lost? Kitchen table day.
Cut the habit of vanishing when plans break,
Replace with minimum viable create—awake
Enough to touch the work. Streak apps lie if they shame—
Your metric is contact, not identical game.
[Bridge]
Life will interrupt—that's not betrayal...
Quitting is the only true derail...
Scale the practice, guard the identity...
You're still a maker in the scarcity.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Book one phone-free hour titled with this line—
If life cuts it short, use the stub. Still thine.
Twyla: scale practice; do not quit the floor—
Tomorrow: minimum session, then once more.
Interrupted? Smaller. Absent? Never.
Scale down. Stay in. Hold the tether.

headline: In the end only the body of work validates
song:
Yo, Twyla Tharp—The Creative Habit—final truth—
In the end only the body of work validates you.
Not the talk, not the brand... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
In the end only the body of work validates,
The stack of finished pieces is what narrates.
Plans and personas fade; the catalog remains,
What you made is the proof that breaks the chains
Of "potential." Potential is a flattering ghost—
Body of work is the host you can toast.
Stop performing the artist; produce the trail—
Years of output tell the only honest tale.
[Verse 1]
Tharp's career is ballets staged, not tweets about grit,
A body of work you can point to: this, then this, then it.
Validation from applause is weather; the oeuvre is climate,
She pushes makers past the self-mythology climate
Of "I'm creative" with empty folders. Receipts matter—
Pieces completed, imperfect, in a scatter
That becomes a path. History judges catalogs:
Shakespeare's plays, not his mood boards; catalogs
Of painters' periods, albums dropped, books in print.
Talk is cheap; the shelf is the mint.
Creative identity without output is cosplay,
Habit plus finished work is the real way.
She won't soothe you with vibes; she'll ask what stands
When the lights go up and the work demands
To exist outside your head. That's the bar—
Body of work. That's who you are.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: portfolio over personal brand lore,
Ship the case studies; fewer posts about the war
Of "building in public" with nothing built.
GitHub commits, lessons taught, zines you stitched,
Products users touch, songs on the service live—
That's validation no bio can contrive.
LinkedIn thought leadership without artifacts? Thin soup.
Kid's wall of drawings over "he's gifted" group
Chat praise. Side hustle: ten ugly versions shipped
Beat one perfect deck that never left the crypt.
AI age: everyone can generate; few can curate
And finish a body that accumulates weight.
This week: cut one habit that fights the body of work—
Endless research, rebranding, scrolling for a perk
Of inspiration. Replace with one finished small thing.
Stack it. Next week, another. Hear the ring
Of real proof. No audience required for the pile to count—
But the pile is what eventually mounts.
[Bridge]
Your name is not the proof...
Your output is the roof...
Under which a life of craft can sleep...
Body of work—what you sow, you keep.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Name the habit that blocks the stack—half it this week,
Finish one piece that could only speak
If "body of work validates" is true in your hands.
Twyla's endgame: not the pose, the stands
Of completed art. Tomorrow: ship something small and real—
Add to the body. That's the deal.
In the end only the body of work validates—
So build the body. Close the gates on empty claims.
Make. Stack. Repeat. Leave a trail they can name.

headline: The source keeps creating — we clear interference to hear it
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin, The Creative Act—source never sleeps—
We clear interference so the signal seeps.
Awareness first... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
The source keeps creating—we clear interference to hear it,
Not force the river; remove the grit, get near it.
Ideas arrive when the noise floor drops,
Less striving, more listening—that's how the work pops.
You are antenna, not only author of force,
Clear the static: ego, rush, the endless course
Of distraction. Source is generous and wide—
Our job is quiet enough to let it ride.
[Verse 1]
Rubin frames the artist as a filter, a receiver,
The universe keeps offering; we choose what to leave or
Block. Interference is taste that's closed too soon,
Fear, agenda, phone, the need to be immune
To weird first drafts. Source doesn't stop at your schedule—
You stop hearing when your life is a meddle
Of constant input. Lineage: meditation rooms,
Painters staring till the subject blooms,
Producers who sit in silence till the song appears
Instead of stuffing tracks with cheap souvenirs.
The Creative Act says: reduce, notice, allow,
Creativity as awareness in the now.
Not laziness—discipline of subtraction,
Clearing space so the real transmission can happen.
Source keeps creating; scarcity is a story we tell
When we're too loud inside to hear the well.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: morning before the feed—two minutes still,
No podcast wall; let one idea arrive at will.
AI is a tool; interference is asking it to think for your soul
Before you've listened. First: notice. Then: enroll
The model as assistant, not as source replacement.
Notifications off for a creative engagement
Block. Walk without earbuds once this week—
Let the street write half the verse you seek.
Work Slack is interference dressed as urgency theater,
Parenting noise is real—so micro-clear: one meter
Of quiet after kids asleep, notebook open blank,
Not "produce," just receive, fill the tank.
Doomscroll is cotton in the antenna's ear—
Cut one scroll session; put awareness here.
Seven mornings: read Rubin's line, one matching act under two—
Clear a little static. Hear what's true.
[Bridge]
Force is a myth of the anxious mind...
Source was speaking the whole time...
Clear interference—humble, slow...
What you need was always in the flow.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: two minutes of nothing before you make,
Then write what showed up—no polish, no fake.
Rick Rubin: source keeps creating; we clear the haze—
Interference down. Reception up. Live that phrase.
Antenna on. Static off. Make from what you hear.
The source is generous. Get out of your own ear.

headline: Audience comes last; truth of the piece comes first
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin in The Creative Act drew the line—
Audience comes last; truth of the piece comes first, every time.
Make it true, then share... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Audience comes last; truth of the piece comes first,
Serve the work before you feed the thirst
Of metrics, trends, the imaginary crowd in your head,
If it's honest, the right people will be led.
Make for the truth in the room, not the algorithm's grin,
Compromised core is how real art gets thin.
Last doesn't mean never—it means sequence, not fame—
Truth first. Audience after. Protect the flame.
[Verse 1]
Rubin produced legends by protecting the song's center,
Not by committee-pleasing every commenter.
When you create for "them" too early, you sand off the weird
That was the reason the piece appeared.
Audience imagining is often fear in costume—
True fans arrive for the undiluted perfume.
Lineage: punk, underground hip-hop, outsider art—
Built for truth, then the crowd found the heart.
Commercial can follow; it shouldn't drive the draft,
First draft is a private vow, not a craft
Of market research. Rubin says the piece knows,
Your job is fidelity until it shows
Itself complete—then release without clinging.
Validation hunting mid-make is what keeps stinging
Creators into blandness. Truth of the piece is the north—
Audience is weather after you set forth.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: draft the newsletter offline, no open rate in mind,
Write the hard paragraph you'd cut to be "kind"
To strangers. TikTok: film the real process before the hook science,
Then package—don't let packaging be the alliance
That starts the work. Startup: build the painful feature users need,
Not the demo candy that only VCs feed.
School creative: one piece you'd stand by if no grade existed,
Parent content: stop performing perfect; post what assisted
Your actual day if you post at all—or don't, and just live.
AI can optimize for engagement; you must give
The constraint: "tell the truth of this piece first."
Phone-free hour titled with that—show up, quench the thirst
For imaginary applause. Make the true version bare,
Then later ask who it's for—with care.
Metrics after meaning. Shares after spine.
Audience last is how the work stays thine.
[Bridge]
The crowd in your head is not the crowd...
They're your fears speaking loud...
Serve the piece; let time sort the rest...
Truth first is how you pass the test.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Book one phone-free hour: truth of the piece, no post,
Even if mood vanishes—protect the host.
Rick Rubin: audience comes last in the order of serve—
Tomorrow make one thing you refuse to swerve
From honesty. Then, if you share, share clean.
Truth first. Audience after. Keep the work keen.

headline: Rules are experiments, not forever handcuffs
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin, The Creative Act—rules on trial—
Rules are experiments, not forever handcuffs, free the dial.
Test, keep, discard... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Rules are experiments, not forever handcuffs,
A scaffold you built—you can change the stuff.
Use a rule till it helps, then question the chain,
What freed you once might now be a drain.
Dogma kills the play; curiosity keeps the door,
Hold methods lightly, hold the work more.
Experiment with constraints, then redesign—
Rules serve the art, not the other way, every time.
[Verse 1]
Rubin invites rules as tools: try writing without rhyme,
Or only rhyme; try silence; try overtime
Limits. The point isn't obedience forever,
It's discovering what unlocks your lever.
Handcuffs are rules you obey from fear or fashion,
Experiments are rules you run like a passion
For learning. Creative deadness often is loyalty
To a method past its usefulness, royalty
To "how I've always done it." Lineage of rebels
Who broke form after mastering it—levels
Of freedom: first accept a container, then revise
The container when the work outgrows the size.
The Creative Act: stay beginner enough to dump
Sacred processes that made your last triumph
But choke your next. Rules are hypotheses dressed
As commandments—undress them. Run the test.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: "I post every day"—try three weeks off,
See if the work deepens or the anxiety coughs.
"No AI in my craft"—try AI for research only,
Then decide with data, not identity lonely.
"I only write at night"—steal a morning and compare,
"We always use this stack"—spike a simpler pair.
School: five-paragraph essay is a training rule—
Later essays can break it when you've got the fuel.
Parenting rules at home: bedtime experiments with care,
Not chaos—structured trials you can share
As a family. Agile at work: ceremonies that help stay,
Retros that kill the ones that waste the day.
Seven mornings: read the line, try one rule flip small—
Not rebellion for clout—science for the call
Of better work. Keep what serves; drop what binds.
Handcuffs off. Experiments on. Free the mind's designs.
[Bridge]
A rule that still serves is a friend...
A rule that freezes you should end...
Test kindly, measure by the work's life...
Not by how tightly you grip the knife.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: name one creative rule you treat as law,
Run a one-day experiment—bend it, see the flaw
Or the gift. Rick Rubin: rules are experiments—live that,
Not forever handcuffs. Adjust the format.
Hold light. Make free. Keep only what still lifts.
Rules on trial. Art gets the gifts.

headline: See yourself without freezing in self-consciousness
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin—The Creative Act—mirror without freeze—
See yourself without freezing in self-consciousness, ease.
Witness, don't paralyze... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
See yourself without freezing in self-consciousness,
Awareness yes, but don't stall the process.
Watch the work and the self with a softer eye,
If you freeze at every glance, the art will die.
Self-consciousness is a spotlight that blinds the stage,
Turn it to a lamp—enough light to engage.
See clearly, keep moving—that's the artist's grace,
Observe without the panic of needing a perfect face.
[Verse 1]
Rubin separates awareness from crippling self-watch,
You need to notice habits, tone, the botch
And the brilliance—but if noticing becomes a statue,
You've mistaken critique for a virtue.
Performers know the mirror can teach or trap,
Too much "how do I look?" and the moment snaps.
Lineage: meditation's witness mind—note and return,
Not spiral. Painters step back, then reenter the burn.
The Creative Act wants honest seeing without the freeze
Of shame mid-stroke. You can course-correct with ease
If you're not defending an identity at war
With the draft. Self-consciousness says "they" are the score;
Healthy seeing says "what's true here?" then continues.
That's the difference between growth and the sinews
Of anxiety dressed as high standards. Unclench—
See, adjust, proceed. Don't freeze on the bench.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: record yourself once, watch once, note three,
Then delete the spiral—edit the work, not the "me."
Zoom camera off for deep work if the face freezes thought,
On for feedback sessions when the seeing is sought
On purpose. Social media drafts: write offline first,
See the piece, not your brand avatar's thirst.
AI mirror: it reflects patterns—use it for craft notes,
Not for existential votes.
Presentation practice: one run for content, one for presence,
Don't run fifteen for self-hate's essence.
Cut the habit of mid-project "who even am I,"
Replace with "what does this paragraph need?"—apply.
Parent filming kids' games: notice joy, not your outfit in the frame,
Creators: feedback groups with rules against character blame.
This week: one session of seeing + continuing—no freeze day—
Catch the freeze, breathe, return to the clay.
[Bridge]
The freeze is fear wearing glasses...
Clear sight can still move the masses...
Witness kindly, then take the next step...
Self-seen and still in mid-leap.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Name the habit that freezes you—half it this week in practice,
Rick's line: see yourself without the self-conscious stasis.
Tomorrow: look once, learn once, keep making past the flinch—
Awareness on. Freeze off. Finish the inch.
See. Don't statue. Move the work along.
That's how seeing serves the song.

headline: Art filters infinite options down to a form
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin, The Creative Act—infinity's a flood—
Art filters infinite options down to a form, draws blood
From choice... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Art filters infinite options down to a form,
Not more ideas—better boundaries in the storm.
Infinity is free; form is a loving no,
You choose what stays so the piece can grow.
Taste is selection under abundance's weight,
Cut is creative; endless is a sedate
Of potential. Commit to a shape, a limit, a frame—
Art is the filter. Form is the name.
[Verse 1]
Rubin sees the world as endless possibility stream,
The artist is the one who dares to dream
And then exclude. Without exclusion, no song—
Just noise. Form is how meaning stays strong.
A sculpture is also all the marble left behind,
A poem is the words you declined.
Lineage: haiku's count, sonnet's cage that frees,
Album sequence, film edit that decrees
What the audience never sees. The Creative Act elevates
Reduction as creation—not a lesser fate.
Infinite options are modern life's default flood;
Without a filter, we drown in "could."
Art says: this, not that—for now, for this piece—
And in that narrowness, the soul finds release.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: Figma with forty frames—pick three to test,
AI with a hundred outputs—crown one, kill the rest
On purpose. Playlist for a road trip: twelve songs, not three hundred liked,
Notion second brain: archive so the active stays tight.
Startup roadmap: three bets this quarter, not thirty maybes,
School project: one thesis, five sources—not a haze of babies
Of half-topics. Parent weekend: one adventure fully done
Beats five tabs of "ideas for fun."
Photography: shoot a lot, show five—the filter is the art,
Email: one ask per message, clarity's the heart.
Make one small finished thing that only exists if filtering is true—
A six-line poem from a page of freewrite you threw
Away mostly. Abundance in, form out—that's the move,
Infinite scroll is the enemy of the groove
Of completion. Close the tabs. Choose the form.
Art is the filter. Weather the infinite storm.
[Bridge]
More is a drug when you're afraid to choose...
Form is a vow you refuse to lose...
Filter with love, not with fear's knife...
Infinite to finite—that's a life.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: take one messy pile, force a form by night—
One page, one sketch, one feature, sealed tight.
Rick Rubin: art filters infinity down—practice the cut,
Commit to a shape. Let the rest stay shut.
Options wide. Form narrow. Ship the chosen few.
That's the creative act—filter through.

headline: Beginner's mind keeps winners from going stale
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin—The Creative Act—stay new on purpose—
Beginner's mind keeps winners from going stale, surface
The wonder again... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Beginner's mind keeps winners from going stale,
Expertise is a tool; arrogance is a jail.
Stay curious like day one, even after the crown,
Ask dumb questions, turn the old ways upside down.
Success can thicken the ears and narrow the eye—
Begin again on purpose so the work doesn't die.
Empty cup on a full resume—that's the flex,
Winners who stay beginners earn the next.
[Verse 1]
Rubin channels Shunryu Suzuki's beginner's mind spirit,
In the expert's mind options are few; in beginner's, infinite merit.
Winning brings formulas that once worked true,
Then the world moves and the formula's through.
The Creative Act warns against becoming a tribute band
To your own past hits. Stay student, stay hand
Open. Lineage: great athletes hire new coaches mid-prime,
Painters switch periods, rappers switch time
Signatures. Stale is optional if humility stays paid.
Mastery without beginner's mind is a parade
For yesterday. Rubin sits with artists at the peak
And invites not-knowing so the real can speak.
Not false naivete—chosen openness as craft,
A disciplined refusal to be trapped by the draft
Of "I already know." That's how winners rot—
Unless they beginner their way out of the spot.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: after a promotion, take a course beneath your tier,
After a viral post, try a form that sparks fear.
AI power user? Spend a week with a tool you mock,
Expert designer? Copy a student exercise on the block
Without your usual tricks. Parent of teens: ask like you don't know
Their world—because you don't fully. Let that show.
Sales quota crushed? Shadow a rookie call, relearn the pitch,
Teacher of twenty years: sit in a peer's class, take notes, switch
One method. Gaming rank high? Play a new genre bad on purpose,
Creative director: make something tiny with zero circus
Of team and budget—hands on again.
Cut the habit of "I've figured the game"—that's the fen
Where stale grows. This week: one beginner session scheduled hard—
No performing expertise, just learning's yard.
Winners go stale when they stop being students of now—
Beginner's mind is the anti-drought vow.
[Bridge]
The trophy can become a mask...
Curiosity is the endless task...
Empty the cup that fame filled tight...
Begin again—that's how you stay bright.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Name one habit that fights beginner's mind—cut it in half,
Then do one clumsy new thing for the craft, not the laugh.
Rick Rubin: beginner's mind keeps winners alive—
Tomorrow, ask a question like you're five
In your field. Stay new. Refuse the stale crown.
Begin again. Write it down. Live it out.

headline: Seeds are everywhere — collect wide, plant few
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin, The Creative Act—seeds on the wind—
Seeds are everywhere—collect wide, plant few—that's the grind.
Gather then choose... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Seeds are everywhere—collect wide, plant few,
Not every spark deserves a greenhouse from you.
Capture freely, commit rarely—that's the wise split,
Abundance of noticing, scarcity of what you commit.
Notebook full of maybes, garden with three true rows—
That's how creators avoid the endless almosts.
Collect wide so life stays rich and strange,
Plant few so something actually can change.
[Verse 1]
Rubin treats ideas as seeds, not finished trees—
You don't owe every seed a full destiny.
Collecting wide keeps awareness fertile and kind,
Planting few respects energy, season, and mind.
The Creative Act loves the pocket notebook energy:
Overhear, observe, stash without the tyranny
Of immediate execution. Later, some seeds sprout
When conditions match—others you throw out
Without guilt. Lineage: composers' motif journals,
Inventors' scrap heaps, directors' visual kernels.
Hoarding projects is planting a forest you can't water;
Collecting seeds is lighter—sort later, slaughter
The weak ones kindly. Wide collection is play;
Narrow planting is how forests actually stay.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: voice memos for every spark on the commute,
Weekly review: plant only two into real pursuit.
Readwise, screenshots, TikTok saves—that's collect wide,
But your active project list stays three max, justified.
AI can brainstorm fifty seeds in a minute flat—
Your job: plant one campaign, not a habitat
Of half-launched brands. School: idea dump before the outline,
Then one thesis planted. Parent: Pinterest of crafts is fine—
Saturday you plant one. Startup icebox vs backlog—same law,
Wide capture, tight commit, less thrash, more awe
Of finished growth. Seven mornings: read Rubin's line,
One matching act under two minutes—capture one seed, decline
To start five. Habit of overplanting is how burnouts bloom;
Habit of collecting without planting is a museum tomb—
Do both sides: wide and few. Balance the botany.
Seeds everywhere. Gardens by choice, not lottery.
[Bridge]
Not every seed is your season...
Collecting is love; planting is reason...
Wide eyes, few roots, deep water...
That's how rare work finds the altar.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: capture three seeds, plant exactly one,
Give it water all week till the week's done.
Rick Rubin: seeds everywhere—collect wide, plant few—
Live the split. Let the garden stay true.
Notebook open. Project list closed and small.
Plant few. Grow tall.

headline: The work tells you what it wants if you listen
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin—The Creative Act—lean in close—
The work tells you what it wants if you listen most.
Not ego's plan only... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
The work tells you what it wants if you listen,
Stop forcing a costume when the piece is glisten-
Ing in another direction—follow the tug,
Collaborate with the thing, not only your smug
Blueprint. Listening is craft, not passive haze,
Iterate till the work settles into its phase.
Ego drafts; the piece replies—hear the reply,
Then change course with humility in your eye.
[Verse 1]
Rubin sits with songs that "want" fewer instruments,
Albums that refuse a single the label invents.
The work has a nature you discover in making,
Not only a brief you wrote at the taking
Of the job. Forcing is how you get polish without soul,
Listening is how a strange true whole
Appears. Lineage: sculptors following the grain of wood,
Writers killing darlings when the story's good
Demands it. The Creative Act: relationship, not dictatorship—
You propose, the piece disposes, you adjust the script.
Silence helps; so does time away and return,
So does playing it for one trusted ear and learn
From what feels alive. The work speaks in friction—
Where it resists your plan, pay attention.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: product users behave "wrong"—maybe the work
Is telling you the real job-to-be-done, not the quirk
You imagined. Novel draft yearns for a shorter form—listen,
App feature nobody uses—stop insisting, glisten
With the data. AI draft keeps drifting to a tone—
Maybe that's a lane; maybe you re-prompt the bone
Of your intent—but notice before you crush.
Phone-free hour: sit with the piece, no rush
To "fix" it—ask what it wants next in plain speech,
Write the answer, try it. Design critique: the mock's reach
Might want less chrome. Song demo: cut the third verse if it sags,
Parent project with kids: the cardboard fort "wants" different bags
Of tape—follow the play. Listening isn't abandoning vision;
It's refining vision with the work in the collision.
Book the hour even if mood is gone—show up, ear open—
The piece talks to people who stop hoping
Only for control. Control softens; dialogue starts.
That's how good work finds its parts.
[Bridge]
Your plan is a guest...
The work is the rest...
Listen between the stubborn lines...
It tells you in signs.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Tomorrow: open one stuck project, ask what it wants—write five lines,
Change one thing it asked for. Cross your designs
With its nature. Rick Rubin: the work tells you—if you listen true—
Phone-free hour. Ear on. Ego quieter in the queue.
Hear it. Follow one tug. Let the piece lead a mile.
The work knows. Sit still. Dial in. Stay a while.

headline: Finishing is practice in letting go
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin, The Creative Act—release the grip—
Finishing is practice in letting go, not a trip
Of perfection... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Finishing is practice in letting go,
Done is a skill; endless polish is a woe
Disguised as standards. Ship, release, unclench the file,
The piece needs a birthday out in the wild.
Holding forever is fear in a craftsman's coat,
Letting go is how the work gets a vote
In the real world. Finish as spiritual reps—
Close the loop. Trust the next. Clear the steps.
[Verse 1]
Rubin knows albums that almost died in eternal mix,
Artists who confuse love with a need to affix
Every possible improvement. Finishing is grief-light—
You mourn the infinite versions in the night
And choose one. The Creative Act frames completion
As practice in non-attachment, not deletion
Of care. You care enough to stop. Lineage: painters who varnish,
Writers who send the manuscript, bands who leave the garnish
Off the twentieth take. Perfectionism is often unfinished business
With self-worth. Finishing says: this version is a witness
Of who I was in this season—enough.
Then the channel clears for the next stuff.
Without letting go, the pipeline clogs with almost-art,
And the artist lives in a museum of false starts.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: hit publish on the post at good, not god-tier,
Merge the PR with tests green—don't sit another year
On pixel dust. Portfolio site v1 live beats v9 in drafts,
Course module shipped to five students teaches faster than crafts
Of forever outlining. AI will always offer one more rewrite—
You decide the stop condition tonight.
Parent photo book: order the print, stop recropping forever,
Side hustle landing page: launch with Stripe, then endeavor
To improve from reality. School paper: submit when the argument stands,
Not when every footnote has spa'd hands.
Phone-free hour titled "finish"—show up, close one loop only,
Even if mood disappears—especially then, lonely
Perfection whispers. Letting go is a rep you can train:
One small finish this week rewires the brain.
Ship the song, the feature, the letter, the sketch—
Finishing is freedom with a final stretch.
[Bridge]
The last 5% can cost 95% of soul...
Know enough; release the whole...
Letting go is love for future work...
Finish. Breathe. Don't lurk.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Book one phone-free hour: finish one thing that lingers—
Rick Rubin: finishing is practice in unclenching fingers.
Tomorrow: define done, meet done, walk away clean,
Trust the next seed. Close the screen on the endless sheen.
Letting go. Shipping out. Making room.
Finish is a practice. Give the work its bloom.

headline: Creativity is a way of being, not only a job title
song:
Yo, Rick Rubin—The Creative Act—beyond the role—
Creativity is a way of being, not only a job title's goal.
Live it awake... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Creativity is a way of being, not only a job title,
Not reserved for artists in a gallery recital.
How you notice, choose, arrange, care, and play—
That's creative life on an ordinary day.
Title or not, the stance is available free:
Awareness, taste, and courage in the small decree.
Don't wait for "creative" on the LinkedIn line—
Be the practice. That's the design.
[Verse 1]
Rubin widens the word: everyone participates in creating
Meals, schedules, conversations, the relating
Of self to world. Job title "artist" is one lane,
Not the whole highway. The Creative Act's refrain:
Creativity as orientation—curious, receptive, bold—
Available to nurses, coders, kids, the old.
Lineage of beginners who never claimed the brand
But shaped beauty and solutions with their hand.
When we gate creativity behind careers and clout,
We starve the daily life of what it's about.
Being creative is attention plus choice plus heart,
Not only invoices from the "creative" part
Of the economy. Rubin invites a lifestyle of noticing—
That's the root; the profession is one blossoming.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: spreadsheet can be elegant design,
Slack message clear is craft. Parent packing lunch is line
And color and care. Commute playlist as a tiny film score,
Teacher's seating chart as choreography of the floor.
You don't need "Founder & Creative" to rearrange a room
For better light. AI tools for anyone—use them to bloom
Ideas, then apply taste. Restate in twelve words max the line,
Run a twenty-five-minute block that treats life as design:
Fix one friction at home, plate dinner with intention, draft
A kinder boundary email—creative craft.
Job title locked in finance or ops? Still collect seeds,
Still filter options, still finish, still meet needs
With original care. Side title optional; way of being required.
No posting needed—just lived, admired
By the people who feel the difference in the day.
Creativity as being. That's the way.
[Bridge]
Title is a hat...
Being is the path...
Anyone can walk it waking...
Make the ordinary your making.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
Restate: creativity is being, not only a job name—
Then one 25-minute block that plays that game
Without performing online. Rick Rubin said it wide—
Live creative: notice, choose, release, abide.
Tomorrow's title might stay the same on paper—
Your way of being can still reshape the vapor
Of routine into something true. Way of being. Start now.
Not only a job title. Take a bow—then begin somehow.
